title: Chilli Chicken Dry

ingredients.name: boneless chicken, cut into 2 cm cubes

subs.name: 500 g paneer or tofu for a vegetarian version
3 tbsp curd for the batter, skip the egg

ingredients.name: cornflour
visual_checks: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry
ingredients.notes: 3 tbsp for batter, 1 tsp for sauce slurry

ingredients.name: soy sauce
visual_checks: 1 tbsp in marinade, 2 tbsp in sauce
ingredients.notes: 1 tbsp in marinade, 2 tbsp in sauce

subs.name: coconut aminos or tamari for a gluten-free option

ingredients.name: ginger-garlic paste
visual_checks: 1 tsp in marinade, 1 tsp for sauce
ingredients.notes: 1 tsp in marinade, 1 tsp for sauce

ingredients.name: spring onion, whites and greens separated

subs.name: 1 tbsp gochujang thinned with a splash of vinegar

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: for deep frying plus 2 tbsp for stir-fry
ingredients.notes: for deep frying plus 2 tbsp for stir-fry

steps.text: In a large bowl combine the chicken with the egg, 3 tbsp cornflour, maida, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, black pepper and salt. Mix well so every piece is evenly coated. Marinate for 15 minutes at room temperature.
Heat oil in a kadai to 175 °C. Lower marinated chicken pieces in batches — do not crowd — and fry for 5 minutes until cooked through and lightly golden. Drain on a rack.

Raise the oil to 190 °C and fry the chicken a second time for 2 minutes until the coating is deeply golden and crisped at the edges. This double-fry is what gives the characteristic crunch. Drain and set aside.
Mix 1 tsp cornflour with 2 tbsp cold water for a slurry. In a small bowl stir together remaining 2 tbsp soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, schezwan sauce and sugar.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok on the highest flame. Add the spring onion whites and green chilli and toss for 20 seconds. Add remaining ginger-garlic paste, then the onion petals and capsicum. Stir-fry for 2 minutes on high heat — the vegetables should char at the edges but stay firm.
Pour in the sauce blend and let it bubble up. Add the cornflour slurry and stir for 30 seconds until the sauce turns thick and lacquered.
Add the fried chicken and toss on high heat for 1 minute until every piece is glazed and the sauce clings tightly. Finish with spring onion greens and serve immediately as a starter or snack.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 500 g paneer or tofu for a vegetarian version (reduce frying time by 1 minute); egg → 3 tbsp curd for the batter, skip the egg (batter will be slightly softer); green-chilli-sauce → 1 tbsp gochujang thinned with a splash of vinegar; soy-sauce → coconut aminos or tamari for a gluten-free option
